todaysEvents.summary: Trump announces conditional 2-week US-Iran ceasefire (Apr 8); Brent crashes -18% to ~$97, WTI ~$97; TTF plunges ~15% to ~€45; iTraxx Asia IG narrows to ~178bp (Apr 9).

Iran claims Hormuz remains closed, citing Israeli strikes on Lebanon (254 killed, 1,165 wounded); VP Vance to lead US-Iran talks in Pakistan on Sat Apr 12; Iran attacks Saudi East-West Pipeline (Apr 9).
India prepares first Iranian oil shipment in 7 years on sanctions relief signal; some vessels begin transiting Hormuz; Gold holds near $4,730 as markets price residual risk (Apr 9).

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Two-week ceasefire holds; Vance Pakistan talks produce a broader framework; Iran formally reopens Hormuz to commercial traffic and lifts Qatari LNG restrictions. P&I insurance gradually restored over 4-6 weeks. Brent consolidates $90-100/bbl; JKM eases to $15-18/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG retraces toward 130-150bp. Kuwait desalination and Saudi East-West Pipeline damage is contained and repaired within weeks.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Ceasefire holds nominally between US and Iran but Israel's Lebanon operations keep Hormuz in dispute. Iran maintains selective Toll Booth access; commercial volumes recover partially but P&I restoration stalls. Vance Pakistan talks produce an interim framework without full normalization. Brent oscillates $90-115/bbl; JKM $17-22/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 160-200bp. Saudi East-West Pipeline disruption limits bypass-route optionality.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Ceasefire collapses within the two-week window as Israel-Lebanon strikes or a new Iranian retaliatory action triggers full resumption of hostilities. Kharg Island strike or Saudi export infrastructure attack brings GCC production back under active threat. Brent re-spikes to $140-185/bbl; LNG spot markets seize; ASEAN recession risk becomes acute. Gold surges past $5,000/oz as dollar safe-haven demand spikes.
